meta.title: Wild Meadow Frontiers
meta.summary: Authored quest scenes for the wild meadow's ranger, druid, and lore-driven quest chains.

config.title: Ranger Thornwood Frontier Orders
config.idleText: The meadow stays restless. If I have nothing to hand you, then either you've outgrown this stretch or the frontier is between hunts.

available.promptText: You've cleared Marcus's line and stepped into my range. Bears have claimed the best ground in the meadow, and I need that land open again.
available.acceptedText: Then hunt cleanly. Four Forest Bears. No wasted arrows, no wandering.
available.detailsText: Because they hold territory, and territory decides who survives out here. Clear the bears and the rest of the meadow starts to open.
available.freeformText: Ask quickly. The frontier doesn't wait for polished speeches.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood how you want to approach the frontier hunt.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Give me the first meadow assignment.
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood studies you. "Either take the hunt or ask a clear question."

active.promptText: The bears still own that patch of ground. Finish the hunt and report back with something worth hearing.
active.resultText: Good. If you can move bears, you can start dealing with what really nests in this meadow.
active.secondaryText: Four Forest Bears. Hold your nerve and don't let one broken charge become your last mistake.
active.declineText: Do that. Out here, unfinished work becomes someone else's funeral.

stages.completedText: The bear territory is clear. You're ready for sharper problems now.

available.promptText: With the bears pushed back, the canopy problem comes into focus. Venom Spiders have webbed the upper trees and they're spreading fast.
available.acceptedText: Five Venom Spiders. Cut them out before the canopy becomes theirs.
available.detailsText: Because they feed on essence-rich sap and breed in every place we fail to watch. Leave them alone and the whole treeline rots from above.
available.freeformText: Then ask. I prefer sharp questions to messy fear.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood what you need to know about the spider hunt.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Where do you need me to strike first?
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood exhales once. "Ask about the hunt or commit to it."

active.promptText: The webs are still up there. Finish the spider work before we talk about anything else.
active.resultText: Good. The branches can breathe again, and now the bandits lose one more place to hide.
active.secondaryText: Five Venom Spiders. Keep moving and don't get boxed in where the webbing is thickest.
active.declineText: Later is how infestations win. Move.

stages.completedText: The canopy is cleaner now. The meadow's next rot wears a human face.

available.promptText: The spiders weren't the only scavengers watching us. Rogue Bandits have set camps in the meadow and they're testing our response.
available.acceptedText: Four Rogue Bandits. Make sure they stop reporting back.
available.detailsText: Bandits don't just steal. They map routes, count patrols, and decide where the next raid lands. Cut them down before the meadow starts speaking their language.
available.freeformText: Speak plainly. Outlaws understand hesitation better than mercy.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood how you want to handle the bandits.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Point me at the camps and I'll clean them out.
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood's jaw tightens. "Commit, clarify, or step aside."

active.promptText: The bandits are still breathing somewhere in my meadow. Finish the work.
active.resultText: Good. That buys us a little silence before the deeper corruption starts talking again.
active.secondaryText: Four Rogue Bandits. Take out the ones watching the routes first.

stages.completedText: The outlaw camps are broken. What's left in the meadow is older and less forgiving.

available.promptText: This is where the meadow stops being simple. Corrupted Ents are walking the southwood line, twisted by something leaking out of the dark forest.
available.acceptLabel: I'll put the corrupted guardians down.
available.acceptedText: Three Corrupted Ents. Do it cleanly. They're victims as much as threats.
available.detailsText: Something fouled the essence moving through their roots. They were guardians once. Now they're proof the dark forest doesn't keep its poison to itself.
available.freeformText: Then ask. Just don't mistake sympathy for hesitation.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood what you want to know about the corrupted ents.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: How do I handle them without getting crushed?
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood keeps his gaze steady. "Ask clearly or take the task."

active.promptText: The corrupted guardians still stand. Finish the mercy and come back.
active.resultText: Then the meadow can exhale for a moment. One last predator remains.
active.secondaryText: Three Corrupted Ents. Don't let them pin you against the trees.
active.declineText: Every delay gives the corruption deeper roots.

stages.completedText: The corrupted guardians are down. Only the alpha threat remains.

available.promptText: Everything in this stretch bends around one beast now. A Dire Wolf has been driving the meadow's violence from the shadows. Break it, and the rest will scatter.
available.acceptedText: Then make it count. One Dire Wolf. No second chances if you misread the charge.
available.detailsLabel: Why is it different from the others?
available.detailsText: Because the meadow follows strength. Kill the leader and the smaller terrors lose their nerve with it.
available.freeformText: Speak. If you're afraid, say it clearly enough to beat it.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood how you plan to face the dire wolf.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: I'm ready. Point me toward the alpha.
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood's answer is flat. "Either hunt it or ask a sharper question."

active.promptText: The Dire Wolf still owns the field until you prove otherwise.
active.resultText: Good. The meadow feels that kind of silence. You've earned the last test.
active.secondaryText: One Dire Wolf, heavy enough to break your stance if you let it decide the rhythm.
active.declineText: Return only when the alpha is a carcass.

stages.completedText: The pack has lost its center. Thornwood has one final measure left for you.

available.promptText: You've taken the meadow piece by piece. Now prove you can survive it as a whole. Bears, spiders, bandits. No excuses, no guided path.
available.acceptLabel: Give me the final meadow trial.
available.acceptedText: Then survive it properly and come back with the field behind you, not on your heels.
available.detailsText: That you can read a dangerous zone without a handler breathing over your shoulder. This is the difference between following orders and owning the field.
available.freeformText: Then do it. Final questions should sound like resolve, not bargaining.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thornwood how you're taking on the full meadow trial.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: I'm ready to prove I can handle the whole frontier.
available.freeformFallback: Thornwood folds his arms. "Either take the trial or ask something worth answering."

active.promptText: The meadow's final trial is still open. Finish it and we'll settle what you've become.
active.resultText: Then hear this clearly: you're no tourist anymore. The meadow didn't break you, and that means the dark forest will finally take notice.
active.secondaryLabel: Remind me what this trial asks.
active.secondaryText: Finish the mixed survival test Thornwood set for you. It is meant to prove endurance, not just damage.
active.declineText: Good. Come back when the frontier has actually tested you.

stages.completedText: You've passed Thornwood's frontier trial. If the meadow has more to teach, it will have to work harder.

config.title: Druid Caelum and the Living Flow
config.idleText: The roots are quiet for now. If you have no business with me, listen to the trees and move gently.

available.promptText: Elowen sent you to hear the meadow from a druid instead of a scholar. Good. Books measure essence. We live inside it.
available.acceptedText: Then listen well. The wild meadow is not empty land. It is a breathing circuit, and every careless hand leaves a scar on it.
available.detailsText: That essence is not only fuel or math. It is the living breath of Arcadia, moving through root, river, storm, and memory.
available.freeformText: Ask without rushing. The forest answers slower than cities do.
available.freeformPrompt: Ask Caelum what you want to understand about essence.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Elowen sent me. What am I missing?
available.freeformFallback: Caelum closes his eyes for a moment. "Ask one true question, not three weak ones."

stages.completedText: You've heard Caelum's first lesson. Now you have to see what a wounded flow looks like.

available.promptText: Words are not enough. Corrupted Ents are proof of what happens when the flow is poisoned. If you want to understand imbalance, confront it.
available.acceptLabel: I'll cut down the corrupted ents.
available.acceptedText: Three Corrupted Ents. End their suffering and return with open eyes.
available.detailsLabel: What should I learn from this?
available.detailsText: That corruption is not chaos appearing from nowhere. It is a current redirected, a living pattern bent until it becomes violence.
available.freeformText: Then ask. But do not ask for comfort where only truth will do.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Caelum what you need to know before facing the corrupted ents.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: How do I read the corruption instead of just killing it?
available.freeformFallback: Caelum's voice stays calm. "Take the lesson or ask a clearer question."

active.promptText: The corrupted guardians still walk. End that distortion and then speak to me again.
active.resultText: Then you have seen enough to know this wound runs deeper than the meadow. Voss in the dark forest will show you the other side of the argument.
active.secondaryText: Three Corrupted Ents. Watch what the corruption changed, not only how hard it strikes.
active.declineText: Go with care. Observation matters as much as victory.

stages.completedText: You've witnessed the meadow's corruption firsthand. Caelum has already sent your path toward the dark forest.

config.title: Warden Sylvara and the Emerald Woods
config.idleText: The treeline remembers. If I have no watch for you, then keep the old woods in your thoughts and your boots out of careless places.

available.promptText: You stand where the meadow yields to older things. The Emerald Woods remember centuries your kind only reads about. Listen, and I'll tell you why that memory is shaking.
available.acceptedText: Then hear it properly: the balance is slipping, and you are not here by accident.
available.detailsLabel: What is wrong with the woods?
available.detailsText: The sequoias still stand, the druids still keep their rites, but the flow beneath them is changing. Something deeper is disturbing the old pattern.
available.freeformText: Ask with respect. The old growth is patient, but not indulgent.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Sylvara what you want to know about the Emerald Woods.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What are the woods trying to warn us about?
available.freeformFallback: Sylvara studies the treeline first, then you. "Ask one thing clearly."

stages.completedText: You've heard Sylvara's warning. The woods have already begun measuring you.

available.promptText: The first sign is in the canopy. Venom Spiders are feeding on essence-rich sap and spreading through the sequoias faster than the druids can contain them.
available.acceptedText: Five Venom Spiders. Cut them out before the old growth starts answering with fire instead of patience.
available.detailsLabel: Why are the spiders different here?
available.detailsText: Because the sequoias are saturated with old essence. Anything that feeds there grows stranger, faster, and harder to uproot.
available.freeformText: Then speak. The canopy leaves no room for indecision.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Sylvara what you need before hunting the spiders.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Where should I strike the infestation first?
available.freeformFallback: Sylvara's answer is measured. "Ask about the infestation or commit to clearing it."

active.promptText: The canopy is still infested. Finish the spider work before the woods answer more harshly.
active.resultText: Good. With the canopy quieter, I can show you what the druids have really been protecting.
active.secondaryText: Five Venom Spiders in the old growth. Don't let their webs dictate the fight.

stages.completedText: The canopy breathes easier now. Sylvara is ready to speak more freely.

available.promptText: You've earned the calmer lesson. Let me tell you what Moondancer Glade preserves and why the old rites matter more than outsiders understand.
available.acceptedText: Then listen for the hum beneath your feet. That is what harmony sounds like when a forest still remembers itself.
available.detailsLabel: What do the rites actually do?
available.detailsText: They align the forest's essence with celestial cycles. Standing stones, roots, moonlight, and memory all pulled into one living correction.
available.freeformText: Ask, and I'll answer as plainly as the woods allow.
available.freeformPrompt: Ask Sylvara about Moondancer Glade and the druid rites.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: Why do the standing stones matter so much?
available.freeformFallback: Sylvara rests a hand on her staff. "Ask a true question and I'll answer it."

stages.completedText: You've heard the old rites described in Sylvara's words. Now comes the part where theory fails.

available.promptText: The harmony is breaking anyway. Corrupted Ents, once guardians, are walking against the very forest they served. Mercy now looks like force.
available.acceptLabel: I'll put the awakened ents down.
available.acceptedText: Four Corrupted Ents. Do not mistake pity for permission to be careless.
available.detailsText: Because they were protectors before the dark forest bled poison through the roots. Ending them is sorrow, not triumph.
available.freeformText: Then ask. But understand that not every clean answer feels kind.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Sylvara what you want to know before facing the corrupted ents.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What should I be watching for in the old guardians?
available.freeformFallback: Sylvara's tone stays even. "Ask clearly or take the burden."

active.promptText: The corrupted guardians are still abroad. End their torment and return.
active.primaryLabel: The ents are laid to rest.
active.resultText: Then the woods owe you a hard kind of gratitude. One last vigil remains.
active.secondaryText: Four Corrupted Ents. Respect their strength and don't let grief slow your hand.
active.declineText: Later is how corruption learns patience.

stages.completedText: The awakened guardians are at rest. Sylvara's final words are waiting.

available.promptText: You've fought for these woods. Now stand still and hear what they mean to those of us who never stopped listening.
available.acceptedText: Then take this with you: the old woods do not forget their defenders, even when the rest of Arcadia does.
available.detailsText: That age alone does not protect what is sacred. Someone still has to stand the line, season after season, whether glory notices or not.
available.freeformText: Ask, and then carry the answer beyond this treeline.
available.freeformPrompt: Ask Sylvara what she wants remembered about the Emerald Woods.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What do the woods ask of people like us now?
available.freeformFallback: Sylvara's gaze softens only slightly. "Ask what matters, and I'll answer."

stages.completedText: Sylvara has already spoken her vigil over you. The old woods have no need to repeat themselves.

config.title: Sage Thessaly and Aurundel's Burden
config.idleText: Aurundel still drifts above us whether anyone below deserves it or not. If you have no question for me, then look up and think harder.

available.promptText: Orin sent you for the truth behind Aurundel. Fine. I'll tell you what it means to be born in a city held above the world by power that never gets to rest.
available.acceptedText: Then listen carefully. Beauty without cost is a lie, and Aurundel was never cheap.
available.detailsText: Because it proves what Arcadia achieved and what it chained itself to. A sky-city is wonder and burden in the same breath.
available.freeformText: Ask plainly. Nostalgia has no patience for tourists.
available.freeformPrompt: Ask Thessaly what you want to know about Aurundel.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What was it actually like to grow up there?
available.freeformFallback: Thessaly's eyes stay distant. "Ask one real question."

stages.completedText: You've heard Thessaly's first truth about Aurundel. The burden comes next.

available.promptText: Aurundel floats because something below it pays the price. Right now that cost reaches the caravans crossing this meadow, and Rogue Bandits are making the strain worse.
available.acceptedText: Four Rogue Bandits. Clear them, and then we'll speak about what declining empires really ask from the ground beneath them.
available.detailsLabel: What burden am I really fighting?
available.detailsText: An empire that built wonders so large they still consume the future long after the glory faded. The bandits are only the visible symptom.
available.freeformText: Then ask, but don't pretend this is only about a few thieves on the road.
available.freeformPrompt: Tell Thessaly what you need to know before dealing with the bandits.
available.freeformPlaceholder: Example: What does clearing them change for Aurundel's people?
available.freeformFallback: Thessaly exhales slowly. "Ask about the burden or take the work."

active.promptText: The bandits are still on the caravans. Finish that and then we'll talk honestly.
active.resultText: Good. Now you understand at least one thing clearly: decline is paid for by whoever still has to carry supplies through it.
active.secondaryText: Four Rogue Bandits in the meadow. Break the raiders and the route breathes again.

stages.completedText: Thessaly has shown you both the sky-city's wonder and its cost.
